page: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv6 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong memory performance, with uncached large-block write memory bandwidth reaching top-tier status at 160.17 GB/sec, and read bandwidth at 337.64 GB/sec. Single-core and multi-core Geekbench scores are strong, supported by high-performance image processing and software compilation capabilities. The server also demonstrates strong database operations, processing 66.87 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Cryptographic and vector extensions, such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enhance its performance in mathematical and inference workloads, as evidenced by strong prompt processing speeds during LLM inference tests.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E96-48s_v6 offers high memory density relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for workloads that require massive memory footprints without needing a proportional scaling of compute cores. This resource allocation minimizes licensing costs tied to vCPU counts while maintaining high memory capacity. The instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, including large relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise resource planning applications, and data analytics pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed. The server is configured with 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmark data highlights top-tier large-block write memory bandwidth alongside strong performance in database operations, Redis SET transactions, and software compilation. Advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX accelerate vector and matrix operations. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads by decoupling high RAM capacity from excessive vCPU counts, thereby optimizing software licensing. It is suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for large-scale database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built for database and in-memory workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring advanced Intel Xeon processing and exceptional memory bandwidth for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed.
768.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio.
Top-tier large-block write memory bandwidth of 160.17 GB/sec.
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX.
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise ERP systems.
